sub_score_definitions.tco: Total Cost of Ownership: explicit annual fees (M&E + rider) plus an estimate of cap-spread drag from constrained equity exposure. Scored 100 at cohort-best drag, 0 at cohort-worst (clamped).
sub_score_definitions.gv: Guarantee Value: Monte Carlo PV of GLWB/GMDB claims divided by PV of rider fees. 50 = neutral (1.0x ratio); 100 = 2.0x or higher. Products without riders default to 50.
sub_score_definitions.sf: Surrender Flexibility: rule-based deduction from 100 for surrender period years, max surrender %, low free-withdrawal corridor, and missing hardship waivers (nursing-home, terminal-illness, disability).
sub_score_definitions.ic: Insurer Credit: AM Best rating mapped to a base score, with deductions for PE ownership (-10) and high Level 3 asset concentration (-5 over 15%, -10 over 25%).
sub_score_definitions.bf: Behavioral Fairness: historical cap-rate cut frequency, illustration-vs-actual delta, NAIC complaint index, and regulatory fines in the prior 5 years. Defaults to 70 when public data is sparse.

regimes.description: Post-Volcker disinflation through dot-com peak. High equity returns, moderate volatility. Pre-1973 stagflation data is unavailable in sp500_monthly_returns.json (history starts 1985-02); this regime is the closest available high-return analogue.
Dot-com bust + Global Financial Crisis. Flat-to-negative cumulative returns, two major drawdowns.
Acute peak-to-trough GFC stress and early recovery. Tests buffer / floor mechanics in a single-regime drawdown.
Low-rate, low-vol bull market. Cap-constrained RILAs may underperform vs uncapped exposure in this regime.
Fed tightening, 2022 equity drawdown, 2023-24 recovery. Tests buffer mechanics in a moderate drawdown plus rapid recovery.

regime_scoring.rationale: Per-regime score (0-100) maps terminal AV to premium: AV >= 2x premium = 100; AV <= 0.5x premium = 0; linear in between. Heuristic — surfaces regime-dependent product behavior without claiming actuarial precision. Reported alongside the rating but NOT included in the composite score (which remains the 5 equal-weighted sub-scores).

notes: Methodology v1.1.0 adds the `regimes` block, which evaluates each product against 5 historical equity regimes (deterministic single-path backtests against actual S&P 500 monthly returns). Regime outcomes are SUPPLEMENTARY DETAIL — they do NOT enter the composite score. The 5 sub-score weights, scoring scenario, and letter bands are unchanged from v1.0.0; the composite is fully backward-compatible. Any change to a weight, sub-score formula, or scoring scenario bumps the major version. Rating output is byte-reproducible from (product spec hash, methodology version, seed).
